```{r lasso_poland_train_zurich_preselect50_fit_and_score, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(TAPSE_sPAP), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_p))

z_sub <- clin_z %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(TAPSE_sPAP), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_z))

y_tr <- ifelse(as.numeric(p_sub$TAPSE_sPAP) < cutoff, 1, 0)
y_te <- ifelse(as.numeric(z_sub$TAPSE_sPAP) < cutoff, 1, 0)

# ---- 3) Build X matrices (Age+Sex unpenalized + proteins penalized) ----
Xtr_prot <- t(as.matrix(data_p[top50, p_sub$Barcode, drop = FALSE]))
Xte_prot <- t(as.matrix(data_z[top50, z_sub$Barcode, drop = FALSE]))

cov_tr <- data.frame(Age = as.numeric(p_sub$Age), Sex = factor(p_sub$Sex))
cov_te <- data.frame(Age = as.numeric(z_sub$Age), Sex = factor(z_sub$Sex, levels = levels(cov_tr$Sex)))

Xtr_cov <- model.matrix(~ Age + Sex, data = cov_tr)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
Xte_cov <- model.matrix(~ Age + Sex, data = cov_te)[, -1, drop = FALSE]

Xtr <- cbind(Xtr_cov, Xtr_prot)
Xte <- cbind(Xte_cov, Xte_prot)

pf <- c(rep(0, ncol(Xtr_cov)), rep(1, ncol(Xtr_prot)))

# ---- 4) CV LASSO ----
set.seed(1)
cvfit_bin50 <- cv.glmnet(
  x = Xtr, y = y_tr,
  family = "binomial",
  alpha = 1,
  penalty.factor = pf,
  standardize = TRUE,
  nfolds = 5,
  type.measure = "auc"
)

# ---- 5) Choose lambda giving 3–10 proteins ----
lambdas <- cvfit_bin50$glmnet.fit$lambda
beta_mat <- as.matrix(coef(cvfit_bin50$glmnet.fit, s = lambdas))
feat_names <- rownames(beta_mat)

prot_names <- colnames(Xtr_prot)
prot_idx <- which(feat_names %in% prot_names)

nprot <- apply(beta_mat[prot_idx, , drop = FALSE] != 0, 2, sum)

ok <- which(nprot >= 3 & nprot <= 10)
if (length(ok) > 0) {
  j <- ok[which.min(cvfit_bin50$cvm[match(lambdas[ok], cvfit_bin50$lambda)])]
  lambda_bin50 <- lambdas[j]
} else {
  j <- which.min(abs(nprot - 5))
  lambda_bin50 <- lambdas[j]
}

beta_use <- as.matrix(coef(cvfit_bin50, s = lambda_bin50))
sel <- rownames(beta_use)[beta_use[,1] != 0]
sel_proteins_bin50 <- sel[sel %in% prot_names]
sel_proteins_bin50

# ---- 6) Predictions for ROC (SSc-PAH only) ----
p_tr_prob <- as.numeric(predict(cvfit_bin50, newx = Xtr, s = lambda_bin50, type = "response"))
p_te_prob <- as.numeric(predict(cvfit_bin50, newx = Xte, s = lambda_bin50, type = "response"))

auc_tr <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(y_tr, p_tr_prob, quiet = TRUE)))
auc_te <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(y_te, p_te_prob, quiet = TRUE)))

roc_tr <- pROC::roc(y_tr, p_tr_prob, quiet = TRUE)
roc_te <- pROC::roc(y_te, p_te_prob, quiet = TRUE)

ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = data.frame(tpr = rev(roc_tr$sensitivities), fpr = rev(1 - roc_tr$specificities)),
            aes(fpr, tpr), color = "red") +
  geom_line(data = data.frame(tpr = rev(roc_te$sensitivities), fpr = rev(1 - roc_te$specificities)),
            aes(fpr, tpr), color = "black") +
  geom_abline(linetype = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "False positive rate",
    y = "True positive rate",
    title = paste0(
      "Top50 preselected LASSO (Age+Sex)\n",
      "Train Poland AUC=", round(auc_tr, 2),
      " | Test Zurich AUC=", round(auc_te, 2)
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```


---

## 2) Compute scores for **ALL patients** (add SSc-noPAH) + 3-group boxplot with p-values

This uses the *same fitted model* (`cvfit_bin50`, `lambda_bin50`, `top50`) and predicts scores for:

- Zurich: SSc-noPAH + SSc-PAH (with TAPSE/sPAP available for SSc-PAH to be grouped)
- Poland: same

```{r lasso_score_allpatients_3groups_boxplot, fig.height=4.8, fig.width=9, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggpubr)

# helper: build X with same sex levels as training
make_X <- function(clin_df, expr_mat, proteins, sex_levels_train) {
  cov_df <- data.frame(
    Age = as.numeric(clin_df$Age),
    Sex = factor(clin_df$Sex, levels = sex_levels_train)
  )
  X_cov <- model.matrix(~ Age + Sex, data = cov_df)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  X_prot <- t(as.matrix(expr_mat[proteins, clin_df$Barcode, drop = FALSE]))
  cbind(X_cov, X_prot)
}

sex_levels_train <- levels(factor(p_sub$Sex))  # from training subset

# Zurich all (SSc-noPAH + SSc-PAH)
z_all <- clin_z %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group %in% c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH")) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_z)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    TAPSE_sPAP = as.numeric(TAPSE_sPAP),
    group3 = dplyr::case_when(
      Group == "SSc-noPAH" ~ "SSc-noPAH",
      Group == "SSc-PAH" & !is.na(TAPSE_sPAP) & TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32 ~ "SSc-PAH <0.32",
      Group == "SSc-PAH" & !is.na(TAPSE_sPAP) & TAPSE_sPAP >= 0.32 ~ "SSc-PAH ≥0.32",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(group3))

# Poland all
p_all <- clin_p %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group %in% c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH")) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_p)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    TAPSE_sPAP = as.numeric(TAPSE_sPAP),
    group3 = dplyr::case_when(
      Group == "SSc-noPAH" ~ "SSc-noPAH",
      Group == "SSc-PAH" & !is.na(TAPSE_sPAP) & TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32 ~ "SSc-PAH <0.32",
      Group == "SSc-PAH" & !is.na(TAPSE_sPAP) & TAPSE_sPAP >= 0.32 ~ "SSc-PAH ≥0.32",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(group3))

group_levels <- c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH ≥0.32", "SSc-PAH <0.32")
z_all$group3 <- factor(z_all$group3, levels = group_levels)
p_all$group3 <- factor(p_all$group3, levels = group_levels)

# predict scores
X_z_all <- make_X(z_all, data_z, top50, sex_levels_train)
X_p_all <- make_X(p_all, data_p, top50, sex_levels_train)

z_prob <- as.numeric(predict(cvfit_bin50, newx = X_z_all, s = lambda_bin50, type = "response"))
p_prob <- as.numeric(predict(cvfit_bin50, newx = X_p_all, s = lambda_bin50, type = "response"))

z_eta  <- as.numeric(predict(cvfit_bin50, newx = X_z_all, s = lambda_bin50, type = "link"))
p_eta  <- as.numeric(predict(cvfit_bin50, newx = X_p_all, s = lambda_bin50, type = "link"))

df_score3 <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  data.frame(cohort="Zurich", Barcode=z_all$Barcode, group3=z_all$group3, prob_low=z_prob, eta=z_eta),
  data.frame(cohort="Poland", Barcode=p_all$Barcode, group3=p_all$group3, prob_low=p_prob, eta=p_eta)
)

comparisons_list <- list(
  c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH ≥0.32"),
  c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH <0.32"),
  c("SSc-PAH ≥0.32", "SSc-PAH <0.32")
)

ggplot(df_score3, aes(group3, prob_low)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ cohort) +
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = comparisons_list,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.format",
    step.increase = 0.09
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "LASSO score (predicted P[Low TAPSE/sPAP])")
```

---

## 3) Cox regression in Zurich SSc-PAH + C-index table

This produces exactly the table you want.

```{r , message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(survival)
library(broom)
library(dplyr)

# Zurich SSc-PAH only, join LASSO eta score
score_z <- df_score3 %>%
  dplyr::filter(cohort == "Zurich") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, eta)

df_surv_z <- clin_z %>%
  dplyr::filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, COMPERA_number, DeathDAYS, Death) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(score_z, by = "Barcode") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    COMPERA_number = as.numeric(COMPERA_number),
    Death_event = ifelse(Death %in% c("yes","Yes","TRUE","1",1), 1, 0),
    DeathDAYS = as.numeric(DeathDAYS)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(DeathDAYS), !is.na(Death_event), !is.na(COMPERA_number), !is.na(eta))

m0 <- coxph(Surv(DeathDAYS, Death_event) ~ COMPERA_number, data = df_surv_z)
m1 <- coxph(Surv(DeathDAYS, Death_event) ~ eta, data = df_surv_z)
m2 <- coxph(Surv(DeathDAYS, Death_event) ~ COMPERA_number + eta, data = df_surv_z)

c0 <- summary(m0)$concordance[1]
c1 <- summary(m1)$concordance[1]
c2 <- summary(m2)$concordance[1]

data.frame(
  model = c("COMPERA", "LASSO score", "COMPERA + LASSO score"),
  c_index = c(c0, c1, c2)
)

# (optional) LRT improvement p-value
anova(m0, m2, test = "LRT")

cox_tables <- bind_rows(
  broom::tidy(m0, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(model="COMPERA"),
  broom::tidy(m1, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(model="LASSO score"),
  broom::tidy(m2, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% mutate(model="COMPERA + LASSO score")
)
cox_tables
```

## 

```{r panel_stability_prepare_data, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(glmnet)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)
library(qs2)

# 0) Make sure TAPSE groups exist (you already do this earlier, but safe)
clin_z <- clin_z %>%
  mutate(
    TAPSE_sPAP = as.numeric(TAPSE_sPAP),
    TAPSE_group = case_when(
      is.na(TAPSE_sPAP) ~ NA_character_,
      TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32 ~ "<0.32",
      TRUE              ~ "≥0.32"
    ),
    TAPSE_group = factor(TAPSE_group, levels = c("≥0.32", "<0.32"))
  )

clin_p <- clin_p %>%
  mutate(
    TAPSE_sPAP = as.numeric(TAPSE_sPAP),
    TAPSE_group = case_when(
      is.na(TAPSE_sPAP) ~ NA_character_,
      TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32 ~ "<0.32",
      TRUE              ~ "≥0.32"
    ),
    TAPSE_group = factor(TAPSE_group, levels = c("≥0.32", "<0.32"))
  )

# 1) Restrict to SSc-PAH for training/testing sets (as in your LASSO code)
train_p <- clin_p %>%
  filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  filter(!is.na(TAPSE_sPAP), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex)) %>%
  filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_p))

test_z <- clin_z %>%
  filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  filter(!is.na(TAPSE_sPAP), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex)) %>%
  filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_z))

# 2) Align proteins between cohorts
common_proteins <- intersect(rownames(data_p), rownames(data_z))
expr_p <- as.matrix(data_p[common_proteins, , drop = FALSE])
expr_z <- as.matrix(data_z[common_proteins, , drop = FALSE])

# 3) Keep only samples we use for model fit/test
expr_p_tr <- expr_p[, train_p$Barcode, drop = FALSE]
expr_z_te <- expr_z[, test_z$Barcode, drop = FALSE]

dim(expr_p_tr)
dim(expr_z_te)

# Define outcome (1 = low TAPSE/sPAP)
y_p <- ifelse(train_p$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)
y_z <- ifelse(test_z$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)
table(y_p); table(y_z)
```


---

## Chunk 2 — stability-selection bootstrap LASSO (Age/Sex unpenalized)

This runs **B=500** bootstraps in **Poland only** and returns:
- selection frequency
- sign stability
- coefficient summaries
- OOB AUC per bootstrap (so you get a “typical performance” distribution without using Zurich)

```{r panel_stability_bootstrap_lasso_functions, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(Matrix)

make_design <- function(expr_mat, clin_df, include_age = TRUE, include_sex = TRUE){
  # expr_mat: proteins x samples, columns match clin_df$Barcode (in same order)
  X_prot <- t(expr_mat[, clin_df$Barcode, drop = FALSE])  # samples x proteins

  cov_parts <- list()
  if (include_age) {
    cov_parts$Age <- as.numeric(clin_df$Age)
  }
  if (include_sex) {
    cov_parts$Sex <- factor(clin_df$Sex)
  }

  if (length(cov_parts) == 0) {
    X <- X_prot
    pf <- rep(1, ncol(X))
    cov_names <- character(0)
  } else {
    cov_df <- as.data.frame(cov_parts)
    # model.matrix handles Sex dummy coding automatically
    X_cov <- model.matrix(~ ., data = cov_df)[, -1, drop = FALSE]  # remove intercept
    X <- cbind(X_cov, X_prot)

    # unpenalize covariates, penalize proteins
    pf <- c(rep(0, ncol(X_cov)), rep(1, ncol(X_prot)))
    cov_names <- colnames(X_cov)
  }

  list(X = X, pf = pf, cov_names = cov_names, prot_names = colnames(X_prot))
}

choose_lambda_by_protein_count <- function(cvfit, prot_names, cov_names,
                                          min_prot = 3, max_prot = 15){
  # choose a lambda that yields between min_prot and max_prot proteins (closest if none)
  lambdas <- cvfit$glmnet.fit$lambda
  beta_mat <- as.matrix(coef(cvfit$glmnet.fit, s = lambdas))

  feat_names <- rownames(beta_mat)
  prot_idx <- which(feat_names %in% prot_names)

  nprot <- apply(beta_mat[prot_idx, , drop = FALSE] != 0, 2, sum)

  ok <- which(nprot >= min_prot & nprot <= max_prot)
  if (length(ok) > 0) {
    # among ok, pick lambda with best CV AUC (min cvm because cv.glmnet stores -AUC? actually for AUC it stores 1-AUC)
    # safer: pick ok lambda that minimizes cvfit$cvm
    j <- ok[which.min(cvfit$cvm[match(lambdas[ok], cvfit$lambda)])]
    return(lambdas[j])
  }

  # if none in range, choose the lambda whose protein count is closest to target mid
  target <- round((min_prot + max_prot)/2)
  j <- which.min(abs(nprot - target))
  lambdas[j]
}

bootstrap_stability_lasso <- function(expr_mat, clin_df, y,
                                     B = 500,
                                     include_age = TRUE,
                                     include_sex = TRUE,
                                     alpha = 1,
                                     nfolds = 5,
                                     min_prot = 3,
                                     max_prot = 15,
                                     seed = 1){
  set.seed(seed)

  # Ensure order consistency
  stopifnot(all(clin_df$Barcode %in% colnames(expr_mat)))
  expr_mat <- expr_mat[, clin_df$Barcode, drop = FALSE]
  y <- as.numeric(y)

  des <- make_design(expr_mat, clin_df, include_age, include_sex)
  X_full <- des$X
  pf <- des$pf
  prot_names <- des$prot_names
  cov_names  <- des$cov_names

  n <- nrow(X_full)
  all_prots <- prot_names

  # storage
  sel_list <- vector("list", B)
  coef_list <- vector("list", B)
  auc_oob <- rep(NA_real_, B)
  lambda_used <- rep(NA_real_, B)

  for (b in seq_len(B)) {
    idx_boot <- sample(seq_len(n), size = n, replace = TRUE)
    idx_oob  <- setdiff(seq_len(n), unique(idx_boot))

    Xb <- X_full[idx_boot, , drop = FALSE]
    yb <- y[idx_boot]

    # CV LASSO on bootstrap sample
    cvfit <- cv.glmnet(
      x = Xb, y = yb,
      family = "binomial",
      alpha = alpha,
      penalty.factor = pf,
      standardize = TRUE,
      nfolds = nfolds,
      type.measure = "auc"
    )

    lam <- choose_lambda_by_protein_count(cvfit, prot_names = all_prots, cov_names = cov_names,
                                          min_prot = min_prot, max_prot = max_prot)
    lambda_used[b] <- lam

    beta <- as.matrix(coef(cvfit$glmnet.fit, s = lam))
    beta <- beta[, 1]
    names(beta) <- rownames(as.matrix(coef(cvfit$glmnet.fit, s = lam)))

    # selected proteins only
    sel_prot <- intersect(names(beta)[beta != 0], all_prots)
    sel_list[[b]] <- sel_prot
    coef_list[[b]] <- beta

    # OOB AUC (if enough OOB samples + both classes)
    if (length(idx_oob) >= 8 && length(unique(y[idx_oob])) == 2) {
      # fit final model on bootstrap sample at chosen lambda, predict on OOB
      fitb <- glmnet(
        x = Xb, y = yb,
        family = "binomial",
        alpha = alpha,
        penalty.factor = pf,
        standardize = TRUE,
        lambda = lam
      )
      p_oob <- as.numeric(predict(fitb, newx = X_full[idx_oob, , drop = FALSE], type = "response"))
      auc_oob[b] <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(y[idx_oob], p_oob, quiet = TRUE)))
    }
  }

  # summarise selection frequency + sign stability
  sel_counts <- table(unlist(sel_list))
  freq <- as.numeric(sel_counts[all_prots])
  freq[is.na(freq)] <- 0
  freq <- freq / B

  # sign stability: among bootstraps where selected, fraction with positive coefficient
  sign_pos <- rep(NA_real_, length(all_prots))
  names(sign_pos) <- all_prots

  for (p in all_prots) {
    signs <- c()
    for (b in seq_len(B)) {
      beta <- coef_list[[b]]
      if (!is.null(beta) && p %in% names(beta) && beta[p] != 0) {
        signs <- c(signs, sign(beta[p]))
      }
    }
    if (length(signs) > 0) {
      sign_pos[p] <- mean(signs > 0)
    }
  }

  res <- data.frame(
    protein = all_prots,
    select_freq = freq,
    sign_pos_frac = sign_pos,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ) %>%
    arrange(desc(select_freq))

  list(
    summary = res,
    sel_list = sel_list,
    coef_list = coef_list,
    auc_oob = auc_oob,
    lambda_used = lambda_used,
    settings = list(B=B, include_age=include_age, include_sex=include_sex,
                    alpha=alpha, nfolds=nfolds, min_prot=min_prot, max_prot=max_prot)
  )
}
```


---

## Chunk 3 — run bootstrap (Poland only) + save objects

```{r panel_stability_bootstrap_run, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
B <- 500

stab_age_sex <- bootstrap_stability_lasso(
  expr_mat = expr_p_tr,
  clin_df  = train_p,
  y        = y_p,
  B = B,
  include_age = TRUE,
  include_sex = TRUE,     # set FALSE if you want "Age only"
  alpha = 1,
  nfolds = 5,
  min_prot = 3,
  max_prot = 15,
  seed = 1
)

qs2::qs_save(stab_age_sex, here::here(output_dir_data, paste0("stability_lasso_B", B, "_AgeSex.qs2")))
head(stab_age_sex$summary, 20)
summary(stab_age_sex$auc_oob)
```


---

## Chunk 4 — plots: selection frequency + OOB AUC distribution

```{r panel_stability_plots, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=9, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
stab_df <- stab_age_sex$summary

# Top selected proteins
topN <- 25
p1 <- stab_df %>%
  slice_head(n = topN) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(protein, select_freq), y = select_freq)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Selection frequency (bootstrap)",
    title = paste0("Stability selection (Poland, B=", B, "): top ", topN, " proteins")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

# AUC distribution (OOB)
auc_df <- data.frame(auc_oob = stab_age_sex$auc_oob) %>%
  filter(!is.na(auc_oob))

p2 <- ggplot(auc_df, aes(x = auc_oob)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25) +
  labs(
    x = "OOB AUC (Poland bootstrap)",
    y = "Count",
    title = paste0("Bootstrap OOB AUC distribution (B=", B, ")")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

p1
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, paste0("stability_top", topN, "_B", B, "_AgeSex.png")),
       plot = p1, width = 9, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)

p2
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, paste0("stability_auc_oob_B", B, "_AgeSex.png")),
       plot = p2, width = 7, height = 4.2, dpi = 300)

# Print helpful summary numbers
cat("OOB AUC mean:", round(mean(auc_df$auc_oob), 3), "\n")
cat("OOB AUC median:", round(median(auc_df$auc_oob), 3), "\n")
cat("OOB AUC 2.5%:", round(quantile(auc_df$auc_oob, 0.025), 3), "\n")
cat("OOB AUC 97.5%:", round(quantile(auc_df$auc_oob, 0.975), 3), "\n")
```


Pick one strategy. I’m giving both:

```{r panel_stability_choose_panel, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
stab_df <- stab_age_sex$summary

# Option A: threshold on selection frequency
freq_thresh <- 0.20  # 40% is strict; try 0.30 too
panel_A <- stab_df %>%
  filter(select_freq >= freq_thresh) %>%
  pull(protein)

# Option B: fixed-size panel
panel_size <- 10
panel_B <- stab_df %>%
  slice_head(n = panel_size) %>%
  pull(protein)

length(panel_A)
panel_A

panel_B
```


---

## Chunk 6 — build **Option 2** (refit logistic regression) + **Option 3** (signed mean z-score)

Key points implemented correctly:
- **Standardize proteins using Poland mean/SD** and apply the same scaling to Zurich
- Provide a function to compute:
  - logistic refit score (eta or probability)
  - signed mean z-score

```{r panel_scores_refit_and_signed, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Choose which panel to use
panel_prots <- panel_A   # or panel_A

# Helper: Poland-based scaling for a set of proteins
get_scaler <- function(expr_mat, proteins){
  m <- rowMeans(expr_mat[proteins, , drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
  s <- apply(expr_mat[proteins, , drop = FALSE], 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  s[s == 0 | is.na(s)] <- 1
  list(mu = m, sd = s)
}

apply_scaler <- function(expr_mat, proteins, scaler){
  Z <- expr_mat[proteins, , drop = FALSE]
  Z <- sweep(Z, 1, scaler$mu, "-")
  Z <- sweep(Z, 1, scaler$sd, "/")
  Z[is.na(Z)] <- 0
  Z
}

# ---- Option 2: refit logistic regression on Poland ----
fit_logistic_refit <- function(expr_mat, clin_df, proteins, cutoff = 0.32,
                               include_age = TRUE, include_sex = TRUE){
  # expr_mat: proteins x samples
  # clin_df order must match columns used
  df <- clin_df %>% mutate(y = ifelse(TAPSE_sPAP < cutoff, 1, 0))
  stopifnot(all(df$Barcode %in% colnames(expr_mat)))

  # Poland scaling inside this cohort (to be re-used later)
  scaler <- get_scaler(expr_mat[, df$Barcode, drop = FALSE], proteins)
  Z <- t(apply_scaler(expr_mat[, df$Barcode, drop = FALSE], proteins, scaler)) # samples x proteins
  Z <- as.data.frame(Z)
  Z$Barcode <- df$Barcode

  dd <- df %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, y, Age, Sex) %>% left_join(Z, by = "Barcode")

  # formula
  rhs <- c()
  if (include_age) rhs <- c(rhs, "Age")
  if (include_sex) rhs <- c(rhs, "Sex")
  rhs <- c(rhs, proteins)
  fml <- as.formula(paste("y ~", paste(rhs, collapse = " + ")))

  m <- glm(fml, data = dd, family = binomial())
  list(model = m, scaler = scaler, proteins = proteins,
       include_age = include_age, include_sex = include_sex)
}

predict_logistic_refit <- function(fit, expr_mat, clin_df){
  df <- clin_df
  stopifnot(all(df$Barcode %in% colnames(expr_mat)))

  Z <- t(apply_scaler(expr_mat[, df$Barcode, drop = FALSE], fit$proteins, fit$scaler))
  Z <- as.data.frame(Z)
  Z$Barcode <- df$Barcode

  dd <- df %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, Age, Sex, TAPSE_sPAP, TAPSE_group, Group) %>%
    left_join(Z, by = "Barcode")

  eta <- as.numeric(predict(fit$model, newdata = dd, type = "link"))
  p   <- as.numeric(predict(fit$model, newdata = dd, type = "response"))

  dd$score_eta <- eta
  dd$score_p   <- p
  dd
}

# ---- Option 3: signed mean z-score ----
signed_mean_score <- function(expr_mat, clin_df, proteins, sign_map, scaler){
  # sign_map: named vector (protein -> +1/-1)
  stopifnot(all(names(sign_map) %in% proteins))

  Z <- apply_scaler(expr_mat[, clin_df$Barcode, drop = FALSE], proteins, scaler) # proteins x samples
  s <- sign_map[proteins]
  Zs <- sweep(Z, 1, s, "*")
  score <- colMeans(Zs, na.rm = TRUE)

  out <- clin_df %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, Age, Sex, TAPSE_sPAP, TAPSE_group, Group)
  out$score_signed <- as.numeric(score[match(out$Barcode, names(score))])
  out
}

# Build sign map from bootstrap results: sign = +1 if mostly positive
sign_map <- stab_age_sex$summary %>%
  filter(protein %in% panel_prots) %>%
  mutate(sign = ifelse(sign_pos_frac >= 0.5, 1, -1)) %>%
  dplyr::select(protein, sign) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

# Fit refit-logistic in Poland (SSc-PAH train only)
refit <- fit_logistic_refit(
  expr_mat = expr_p,
  clin_df  = train_p,
  proteins = panel_prots,
  cutoff = 0.32,
  include_age = TRUE,
  include_sex = TRUE
)

# Predict in Poland SSc-PAH (train) and Zurich SSc-PAH (test)
pred_p <- predict_logistic_refit(refit, expr_p, train_p)
pred_z <- predict_logistic_refit(refit, expr_z, test_z)

# Signed score uses the SAME Poland scaler as refit (so fully portable)
signed_p <- signed_mean_score(expr_p, train_p, panel_prots, sign_map, refit$scaler)
signed_z <- signed_mean_score(expr_z, test_z, panel_prots, sign_map, refit$scaler)

head(pred_z)
head(signed_z)
```


---

## Chunk 7 — evaluate in Zurich: ROC + your 3-group boxplot

### ROC for SSc-PAH-only test set (Zurich)
```{r panel_scores_eval_roc, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=5.2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Outcomes
y_tr <- ifelse(pred_p$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)
y_te <- ifelse(pred_z$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)

# AUCs (refit logistic)
roc_tr <- pROC::roc(y_tr, pred_p$score_p, quiet = TRUE)
roc_te <- pROC::roc(y_te, pred_z$score_p, quiet = TRUE)

auc_tr <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_tr))
auc_te <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_te))

plot(roc_tr, col="red",
     main=paste0("Stable panel refit-logistic\nTrain Poland AUC=", round(auc_tr,2),
                 " | Test Zurich AUC=", round(auc_te,2)))
lines(roc_te, col="black")
abline(a=0, b=1, lty=2)
legend("bottomright", legend=c("Poland (train)", "Zurich (test)"),
       col=c("red","black"), lwd=2, bty="n")

ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, paste0("stablePanel_refitLogistic_ROC.png")),
       width = 5.2, height = 3.8, dpi = 300)
```
       

### 3-group boxplot (SSc-noPAH, SSc-PAH≥0.32, SSc-PAH<0.32)
This mimics your figure logic but uses the **new score**. We apply the score to **all patients** in each cohort (not only SSc-PAH), so you can show separation from SSc-noPAH as well.

```{r panel_scores_boxplot_3groups, fig.height=4.8, fig.width=9, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggpubr)

# Build 3-group labels for plotting
make_3groups <- function(clin_df){
  clin_df %>%
    mutate(
      group3 = case_when(
        Group == "SSc-noPAH" ~ "SSc-noPAH",
        Group == "SSc-PAH" & TAPSE_sPAP >= 0.32 ~ "SSc-PAH≥0.32",
        Group == "SSc-PAH" & TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32  ~ "SSc-PAH<0.32",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      group3 = factor(group3, levels = c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH≥0.32", "SSc-PAH<0.32"))
    )
}

# Score ALL subjects in each cohort (use refit logistic)
clin_p_all <- clin_p %>% filter(Barcode %in% colnames(expr_p), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex))
clin_z_all <- clin_z %>% filter(Barcode %in% colnames(expr_z), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex))

pred_p_all <- predict_logistic_refit(refit, expr_p, clin_p_all)
pred_z_all <- predict_logistic_refit(refit, expr_z, clin_z_all)

pred_p_all <- make_3groups(pred_p_all)
pred_z_all <- make_3groups(pred_z_all)

df_plot <- bind_rows(
  pred_p_all %>% mutate(Cohort = "Poland"),
  pred_z_all %>% mutate(Cohort = "Zurich")
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(group3))

# Boxplot of probability score (predicted P[Low TAPSE/sPAP])
p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = group3, y = score_p)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.3, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~Cohort, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Score (predicted P[Low TAPSE/sPAP])",
       title = paste0("Stable panel refit-logistic score (", length(panel_prots), " proteins)")) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))

p
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, paste0("stablePanel_refitLogistic_3groups_boxplot.png")),
       plot = p, width = 9, height = 4.8, dpi = 300)
```
       

---

## Optional (but very useful) — same evaluation for **signed mean z-score**
If you want the “super clinic-friendly index” plot/ROC too:

```{r panel_scores_signed_eval, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=5.2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ROC on SSc-PAH-only (Zurich test)
y_te <- ifelse(signed_z$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)
roc_signed <- pROC::roc(y_te, signed_z$score_signed, quiet = TRUE)
auc_signed <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_signed))

plot(roc_signed,
     main=paste0("Signed mean z-score (", length(panel_prots), " proteins)\nZurich AUC=", round(auc_signed,2)))
abline(a=0, b=1, lty=2)
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, paste0("stablePanel_signedMeanZ_ROC.png")),
       width = 5.2, height = 3.8, dpi = 300)
```


## Final lasso

```{r preselect_proteins_DE_Poland, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(limma)
library(dplyr)

# Poland samples for DE (PAH vs noPAH)
de_meta_p <- clin_p %>%
  filter(Group %in% c("SSc-PAH", "SSc-noPAH")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex)) %>%
  filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_p))

X_de <- as.matrix(data_p[, de_meta_p$Barcode, drop = FALSE])

# Design: Group + Age + Sex
de_meta_p$Group <- factor(de_meta_p$Group, levels = c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH"))
de_meta_p$Sex   <- factor(de_meta_p$Sex)

design <- model.matrix(~ Group + Age + Sex, data = de_meta_p)

fit <- lmFit(X_de, design)
fit <- eBayes(fit)

DE_pah_vs_nopah <- topTable(fit, coef = "GroupSSc-PAH", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
DE_pah_vs_nopah$protein <- rownames(DE_pah_vs_nopah)

# Choose your filter (match your original logic)
# Example: adj.P.Val < 0.05 (optionally add |logFC| cutoff)
preselect_proteins <- DE_pah_vs_nopah %>%
  dplyr::filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05) %>%
 # dplyr::filter(protein %in% dplyr::filter(DE_p_bin, P.Value < 0.05)$Gene) %>%
  pull(protein)

length(preselect_proteins)
head(preselect_proteins)

qs2::qs_save(DE_pah_vs_nopah, here::here(output_dir_data, "DE_Poland_SScPAH_vs_SScnoPAH_AgeSex.qs2"))
qs2::qs_save(preselect_proteins, here::here(output_dir_data, "preselect_proteins_DEadjP005.qs2"))

```


> If you want to mimic your “Top50 preselect”, replace the filter with `slice_min(adj.P.Val, n = 50)` (or rank by your score).

---

# Chunk B — re-run the stability-selection LASSO using only the preselected proteins

This is the **only change** you need: restrict `expr_p_tr` and `expr_z_te` to the preselected proteins (and keep the intersection with Zurich).


```{r stability_lasso_with_preselection, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Intersect with proteins available in BOTH cohorts
common_preselect <- intersect(preselect_proteins, intersect(rownames(data_p), rownames(data_z)))
length(common_preselect)

# Build training/test expression matrices for LASSO (proteins x samples)
expr_p_tr_pre <- as.matrix(data_p[common_preselect, train_p$Barcode, drop = FALSE])
expr_z_te_pre <- as.matrix(data_z[common_preselect, test_z$Barcode, drop = FALSE])

# Run bootstrap stability selection on the preselected feature space
B <- 500
stab_pre <- bootstrap_stability_lasso(
  expr_mat = expr_p_tr_pre,
  clin_df  = train_p,
  y        = y_p,
  B = B,
  include_age = TRUE,
  include_sex = TRUE,    # or FALSE if you want Age-only adjustment
  alpha = 1,
  nfolds = 5,
  min_prot = 3,
  max_prot = 100,
  seed = 1
)

qs2::qs_save(stab_pre, here::here(output_dir_data, paste0("stability_lasso_preselected_B", B, "_AgeSex.qs2")))
head(stab_pre$summary, 20)
```


---



```{r stability_panel_size_distribution, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# number of proteins selected per bootstrap
panel_sizes <- sapply(stab_pre$sel_list, length)

df_sizes <- data.frame(size = panel_sizes)

p_size <- ggplot(df_sizes, aes(x = size)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(panel_sizes))) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of proteins selected (per bootstrap)",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Stability selection: distribution of panel sizes"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

p_size
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, paste0("stability_panel_size_distribution_B", stab_pre$settings$B, ".png")),
       p_size, width = 6, height = 4.2, dpi = 300)

summary(panel_sizes)
table(panel_sizes)
```


---

## 2) “Most frequent combination” (top recurring panels)
This is useful, but usually you’ll see many singletons unless you restrict to a manageable feature universe. So we compute frequency of **exact sets**.

```{r stability_most_frequent_panels, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tibble)

panel_keys <- sapply(stab_pre$sel_list, function(v){
  if (length(v) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  paste(sort(v), collapse = " | ")
})

panel_tab <- sort(table(panel_keys), decreasing = TRUE)
panel_df <- data.frame(
  panel = names(panel_tab),
  n = as.integer(panel_tab),
  freq = as.integer(panel_tab) / length(stab_pre$sel_list),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(panel))

head(panel_df, 20)
```


**Optional plot (top 10 panels):**
```{r stability_most_frequent_panels_plot, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=12, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
topM <- 10
p_panels <- panel_df %>%
  slice_head(n = topM) %>%
  mutate(panel = factor(panel, levels = rev(panel))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = panel, y = freq)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Frequency", title = paste0("Top ", topM, " most frequent exact panels")) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

p_panels
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, paste0("stability_top", topM, "_panels_B", stab_pre$settings$B, ".png")),
       p_panels, width = 9, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
```


---

## 3) Co-selection heatmap (which proteins tend to appear together)
This is *often more informative* than exact combinations.

```{r stability_co_selection_heatmap, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=8, fig.width=8}
library(Matrix)

# pick a manageable set (e.g. top 30 by selection freq)
topK <- 30
top_prots <- stab_pre$summary %>% slice_head(n = topK) %>% pull(protein)

# build co-selection matrix
M <- matrix(0, nrow = topK, ncol = topK, dimnames = list(top_prots, top_prots))

for (sel in stab_pre$sel_list) {
  sel <- intersect(sel, top_prots)
  if (length(sel) >= 2) {
    idx <- match(sel, top_prots)
    M[idx, idx] <- M[idx, idx] + 1
  } else if (length(sel) == 1) {
    i <- match(sel, top_prots)
    M[i, i] <- M[i, i] + 1
  }
}

# convert to frequency
M_freq <- M / length(stab_pre$sel_list)

# quick base heatmap
heatmap(M_freq, symm = TRUE, margins = c(8, 8),
        main = paste0("Co-selection frequency (top ", topK, " proteins)"))
```
        

       
# Chunk C — build final panel + refit-logistic + signed score (using preselection results)

```{r build_scores_from_stability_preselected, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
stab_df <- stab_pre$summary

# pick your panel
panel_size <- 5
panel_prots <- stab_df %>% slice_head(n = panel_size) %>% pull(protein)
panel_prots

# sign map from bootstrap
sign_map <- stab_df %>%
  filter(protein %in% panel_prots) %>%
  mutate(sign = ifelse(sign_pos_frac >= 0.5, 1, -1)) %>%
  dplyr::select(protein, sign) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

# IMPORTANT: refit/scores must use the FULL cohort matrices (data_p/data_z)
# but only these proteins
refit_pre <- fit_logistic_refit(
  expr_mat = as.matrix(data_p),  # proteins x samples
  clin_df  = train_p,
  proteins = panel_prots,
  cutoff = 0.32,
  include_age = TRUE,
  include_sex = TRUE
)

pred_p <- predict_logistic_refit(refit_pre, as.matrix(data_p), train_p)
pred_z <- predict_logistic_refit(refit_pre, as.matrix(data_z), test_z)

signed_p <- signed_mean_score(as.matrix(data_p), train_p, panel_prots, sign_map, refit_pre$scaler)
signed_z <- signed_mean_score(as.matrix(data_z), test_z, panel_prots, sign_map, refit_pre$scaler)
```


---

# Chunk D — FIXED plotting: ROC + boxplots 

## D1) ROC (same as before)

```{r roc_refit_preselected, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=5.2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
y_tr <- ifelse(pred_p$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)
y_te <- ifelse(pred_z$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)

roc_tr <- pROC::roc(y_tr, pred_p$score_p, quiet = TRUE)
roc_te <- pROC::roc(y_te, pred_z$score_p, quiet = TRUE)

auc_tr <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_tr))
auc_te <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_te))

plot(roc_tr, col="red",
     main=paste0("Stable panel refit-logistic\nTrain Poland AUC=", round(auc_tr,2),
                 " | Test Zurich AUC=", round(auc_te,2)))
lines(roc_te, col="black")
abline(a=0, b=1, lty=2)
legend("bottomright", legend=c("Poland (train)", "Zurich (test)"),
       col=c("red","black"), lwd=2, bty="n")
```


## D2) Better “3-group” plot: use **linear predictor** (`score_eta`) not probability
Also: it’s more defensible to show this plot **within PAH** (your prediction task), and keep “noPAH” only as exploratory.

```{r boxplot_eta_preselected, fig.height=4.8, fig.width=9, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggpubr)

make_3groups <- function(clin_df){
  clin_df %>%
    mutate(
      group3 = case_when(
        Group == "SSc-noPAH" ~ "SSc-noPAH",
        Group == "SSc-PAH" & TAPSE_sPAP >= 0.32 ~ "SSc-PAH≥0.32",
        Group == "SSc-PAH" & TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32  ~ "SSc-PAH<0.32",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      group3 = factor(group3, levels = c("SSc-noPAH", "SSc-PAH≥0.32", "SSc-PAH<0.32"))
    )
}

# score ALL subjects to visualize, but plot ETA
clin_p_all <- clin_p %>% filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_p), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex))
clin_z_all <- clin_z %>% filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_z), !is.na(Age), !is.na(Sex))

pred_p_all <- predict_logistic_refit(refit_pre, as.matrix(data_p), clin_p_all) %>% make_3groups()
pred_z_all <- predict_logistic_refit(refit_pre, as.matrix(data_z), clin_z_all) %>% make_3groups()

df_plot <- bind_rows(
  pred_p_all %>% mutate(Cohort = "Poland"),
  pred_z_all %>% mutate(Cohort = "Zurich")
) %>% filter(!is.na(group3))

p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = group3, y = score_eta)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.3, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~Cohort, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Score (log-odds; linear predictor)",
       title = paste0("Stable panel refit-logistic score (", length(panel_prots), " proteins)")) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))

p
```

## Correlations

```{r corr_prepare_pah_only, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

panel_prots <- c("CLSTN2","SIGLEC8","LRRN1","CXCL17","IL15")

# helper to build a long dataframe for correlations
make_corr_df <- function(clin_df, data_mat, cohort_label, score_df = NULL,
                         score_var = c("score_eta","score_p","score_signed")) {
  score_var <- match.arg(score_var)

  # subset to SSc-PAH with TAPSE/sPAP
  cc <- clin_df %>%
    filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
    filter(!is.na(TAPSE_sPAP)) %>%
    filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_mat))

  # protein values (wide)
  prot_wide <- t(as.matrix(data_mat[panel_prots, cc$Barcode, drop = FALSE])) %>%
    as.data.frame()
  prot_wide$Barcode <- cc$Barcode

  out <- cc %>%
    dplyr::select(Barcode, TAPSE_sPAP) %>%
    left_join(prot_wide, by = "Barcode") %>%
    mutate(Cohort = cohort_label)

  # attach score if provided
  if (!is.null(score_df)) {
    out <- out %>%
      left_join(score_df %>% dplyr::select(Barcode, all_of(score_var)), by = "Barcode") %>%
      rename(Score = all_of(score_var))
  } else {
    out$Score <- NA_real_
  }

  out
}

# Use your previously created score data frames:
# pred_p, pred_z contain score_eta and score_p for SSc-PAH train/test
# signed_p, signed_z contain score_signed if you want that too

df_corr_p <- make_corr_df(train_p, data_p, "Poland", score_df = pred_p, score_var = "score_eta")
df_corr_z <- make_corr_df(test_z,  data_z, "Zurich", score_df = pred_z, score_var = "score_eta")

# Combine
df_corr <- bind_rows(df_corr_p, df_corr_z)

# quick sanity check
df_corr %>% group_by(Cohort) %>% summarise(n = n())
```

```{r corr_table_proteins_and_score, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
cor_spearman <- function(x, y) {
  ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y)
  if (sum(ok) < 5) return(c(rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = sum(ok)))
  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x[ok], y[ok], method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
  c(rho = unname(ct$estimate), p = ct$p.value, n = sum(ok))
}

features <- c(panel_prots, "Score")

corr_tbl <- df_corr %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(panel_prots), names_to = "Feature", values_to = "Value") %>%
  # add Score as another feature
  bind_rows(
    df_corr %>% transmute(Cohort, Barcode, TAPSE_sPAP, Feature = "Score", Value = Score)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Cohort, Feature) %>%
  summarise(
    rho = cor_spearman(Value, TAPSE_sPAP)["rho"],
    p   = cor_spearman(Value, TAPSE_sPAP)["p"],
    n   = cor_spearman(Value, TAPSE_sPAP)["n"],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Cohort) %>%
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p, method = "BH")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(Cohort, p_adj)

corr_tbl
qs2::qs_save(corr_tbl, here::here(output_dir_data, "corr_TAPSEsPAP_panelProteins_and_score_Spearman.qs2"))
write.csv(corr_tbl, here::here(output_dir_data, "corr_TAPSEsPAP_panelProteins_and_score_Spearman.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r corr_plot_score_vs_tapse, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=7.5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# pull rho/p for annotation
ann_score <- corr_tbl %>% filter(Feature == "Score") %>% dplyr::select(Cohort, rho, p, p_adj)

df_score <- df_corr %>%
  filter(!is.na(Score)) %>%
  left_join(ann_score, by = "Cohort")

p_score <- ggplot(df_score, aes(x = TAPSE_sPAP, y = Score)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~Cohort, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "TAPSE/sPAP",
    y = "Score (linear predictor, log-odds)",
    title = "Score vs TAPSE/sPAP in SSc-PAH"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

# Add annotation per facet (simple approach)
p_score <- p_score +
  geom_text(
    data = df_score %>% distinct(Cohort, rho, p, p_adj),
    aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf,
        label = paste0("Spearman ρ=", round(rho, 2),
                       "\np=", signif(p, 2))),
    hjust = -1, vjust = 1.1,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  )

p_score
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "corr_score_vs_TAPSEsPAP_SScPAH.png"),
       p_score, width = 7.5, height = 3.8, dpi = 300)
```

```{r corr_plot_proteins_vs_tapse_faceted, fig.height=5, fig.width=10.5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# prepare long data for plotting
df_long <- df_corr %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(panel_prots), names_to = "Protein", values_to = "NPX") %>%
  left_join(
    corr_tbl %>% filter(Feature %in% panel_prots) %>%
      rename(Protein = Feature) %>%
      dplyr::select(Cohort, Protein, rho, p, p_adj),
    by = c("Cohort", "Protein")
  )

p_prot <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = TAPSE_sPAP, y = NPX)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(Cohort ~ Protein, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "TAPSE/sPAP",
    y = "Protein NPX",
    title = "Individual proteins vs TAPSE/sPAP in SSc-PAH"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )

# annotate each facet with rho and FDR
ann_df <- df_long %>%
  distinct(Cohort, Protein, rho, p_adj, p)

p_prot <- p_prot +
  geom_text(
    data = ann_df,
    aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf,
        label = paste0("ρ=", round(rho, 2), "\np=", signif(p, 2))),
    hjust = -1, vjust = 1.1,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3
  )

p_prot
ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "corr_proteins_vs_TAPSEsPAP_SScPAH_faceted.png"),
       p_prot, width = 10.5, height = 7.5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r zurich_withincohort_scaling_auc, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=5.2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(pROC)

panel_prots <- c("CLSTN2","SIGLEC8","LRRN1","CXCL17","IL15")

# If you already have sign_map from your stability selection, keep it.
# Otherwise, derive it from stab_pre$summary:
# sign_map <- stab_pre$summary %>%
#   filter(protein %in% panel_prots) %>%
#   mutate(sign = ifelse(sign_pos_frac >= 0.5, 1, -1)) %>%
#   select(protein, sign) %>%
#   tibble::deframe()

# --- Zurich SSc-PAH only ---
z_pah <- clin_z %>%
  filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  filter(!is.na(TAPSE_sPAP)) %>%
  filter(Barcode %in% colnames(data_z))

# outcome
y_z <- ifelse(z_pah$TAPSE_sPAP < 0.32, 1, 0)

# protein matrix: proteins x samples
Zmat <- as.matrix(data_z[panel_prots, z_pah$Barcode, drop = FALSE])

# Zurich-within-cohort z-score per protein (center/scale across Zurich SSc-PAH samples)
mu_z <- rowMeans(Zmat, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_z <- apply(Zmat, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_z[sd_z == 0 | is.na(sd_z)] <- 1

Zz <- sweep(Zmat, 1, mu_z, "-")
Zz <- sweep(Zz,   1, sd_z, "/")
Zz[is.na(Zz)] <- 0

# signed mean z-score (higher should correspond to "worse" depending on sign_map)
s <- sign_map[panel_prots]
Zsigned <- sweep(Zz, 1, s, "*")
score_withinZ <- colMeans(Zsigned, na.rm = TRUE)

# ROC / AUC
roc_withinZ <- roc(y_z, as.numeric(score_withinZ), quiet = TRUE)
auc_withinZ <- as.numeric(auc(roc_withinZ))

plot(roc_withinZ,
     main = paste0("Zurich within-cohort scaled signed score\nAUC = ", round(auc_withinZ, 2)))
abline(a=0, b=1, lty=2)

ggsave(here::here(output_dir_figs, "AUC_Zurich_withinCohortScaling_signedScore.png"),
       width = 5.2, height = 3.8, dpi = 300)
```

## COX regression

```{r cox_prepare_surv_zurich, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(survival)

# -------------------------------
# 0) Choose which score to use
# -------------------------------
score_z <- pred_z %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, eta = score_eta)   # use linear predictor (recommended)

# -------------------------------
# 1) Robust survival column picker
# -------------------------------
pick_first_existing <- function(df, candidates) {
  hit <- candidates[candidates %in% colnames(df)]
  if (length(hit) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  hit[1]
}

# Try to guess your column names (adjust here if needed)
time_col  <- pick_first_existing(clin_z, c("DeathDAYS","death_days","FU_DAYS","followup_days","time_days","TimeDAYS","Time_days"))
event_col <- pick_first_existing(clin_z, c("Death","death","Death_event","event","Event","status","Status"))

# COMPERA numeric column (you mentioned COMPERA_number)
compera_col <- pick_first_existing(clin_z, c("COMPERA_number","COMPERA","COMPERA_num","COMPERA_score"))

cat("Using time_col =", time_col, "\n")
cat("Using event_col =", event_col, "\n")
cat("Using compera_col =", compera_col, "\n")

# If any are NA, set them manually like:
# time_col <- "DeathDAYS"
# event_col <- "Death"
# compera_col <- "COMPERA_number"

stopifnot(!is.na(time_col), !is.na(event_col), !is.na(compera_col))

# -------------------------------
# 2) Build Zurich SSc-PAH survival dataframe
# -------------------------------
df_surv_z <- clin_z %>%
  filter(Group == "SSc-PAH") %>%
  dplyr::select(Barcode, all_of(compera_col), all_of(time_col), all_of(event_col)) %>%
  left_join(score_z, by = "Barcode") %>%
  mutate(
    # numeric COMPERA
    COMPERA_number = as.numeric(.data[[compera_col]]),

    # time in days
    time_days = as.numeric(.data[[time_col]]),

    # event coding: try to handle yes/no, TRUE/FALSE, 1/0
    event_raw = .data[[event_col]],
    event = case_when(
      is.numeric(event_raw) ~ ifelse(event_raw > 0, 1, 0),
      is.logical(event_raw) ~ ifelse(event_raw, 1, 0),
      tolower(as.character(event_raw)) %in% c("yes","y","true","1","dead","death") ~ 1,
      tolower(as.character(event_raw)) %in% c("no","n","false","0","alive") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(time_days), !is.na(event), !is.na(COMPERA_number), !is.na(eta))

# quick check
df_surv_z %>% summarise(n=n(), events=sum(event), median_time=median(time_days))
table(df_surv_z$event)
```


---

## Chunk 2 — Cox models + c-index table (+ LRT)

```{r cox_zurich_cindex_table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(survival)

m0 <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ COMPERA_number, data = df_surv_z, x = TRUE)
m1 <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ eta,            data = df_surv_z, x = TRUE)
m2 <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ COMPERA_number + eta, data = df_surv_z, x = TRUE)

c0 <- unname(summary(m0)$concordance[1])
c1 <- unname(summary(m1)$concordance[1])
c2 <- unname(summary(m2)$concordance[1])

cindex_tbl <- data.frame(
  model   = c("COMPERA", "Score", "COMPERA + score"),
  c_index = c(c0, c1, c2)
)

cindex_tbl

# Likelihood ratio test: does adding score improve COMPERA?
lrt <- anova(m0, m2, test = "LRT")
lrt
```


This gives you exactly the table format you showed (just with “Score” instead of “LASSO score” — rename if you want).

---

## Optional (recommended) — bootstrap CI for c-index difference (COMPERA vs COMPERA+score)

With small cohorts/events, c-index can be noisy. This gives you a quick sense of stability.

```{r cox_zurich_cindex_bootstrap, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
set.seed(1)
B <- 500

boot_delta <- replicate(B, {
  idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(df_surv_z)), replace = TRUE)
  d <- df_surv_z[idx, , drop = FALSE]

  # handle rare bootstrap samples with 0 events
  if (length(unique(d$event)) < 2) return(NA_real_)

  m0b <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ COMPERA_number, data = d)
  m2b <- coxph(Surv(time_days, event) ~ COMPERA_number + eta, data = d)

  c0b <- unname(summary(m0b)$concordance[1])
  c2b <- unname(summary(m2b)$concordance[1])
  c2b - c0b
})

boot_delta <- boot_delta[is.finite(boot_delta)]
quantile(boot_delta, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))
mean(boot_delta)
```


---

## Optional — model summary table (HRs)
If you want a quick HR table for the combined model:

```{r cox_zurich_hr_table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(broom)

tidy(m2, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)
```

```{r plot_boot_delta, fig.height=3.6, fig.width=5.5}
library(ggplot2)
df_bd <- data.frame(delta_c = boot_delta)

ggplot(df_bd, aes(x = delta_c)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  labs(x = "Δ C-index (COMPERA+score − COMPERA)",
       y = "Count",
       title = "Bootstrap distribution of c-index improvement") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

```

```{r}
cox.zph(m2)
```
